data.title: Skyfame Realty Announces Adjournment of Winding-Up Petition Hearing
data.description:
The latest update is out from Skyfame Realty (Holdings) Ltd ( (HK:0059) ).

Skyfame Realty (Holdings) Limited, a company incorporated in Bermuda, has announced the adjournment of a court hearing related to a petition for its winding up. The hearing, initially scheduled in the Commercial Court of the Supreme Court of Bermuda, has been postponed to November 14, 2025. This development is part of the company’s ongoing restructuring efforts, with provisional liquidators appointed to oversee the process. Shareholders and potential investors are advised to exercise caution when dealing with the company’s securities.

More about Skyfame Realty (Holdings) Ltd

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: HK$84.46M

data.title: Ventia Secures $935 Million Defence Clothing Services Contract
data.description:
Ventia Services Group Limited ( (AU:VNT) ) just unveiled an update.

Ventia Services Group Ltd has secured a significant contract with the Australian Defence Force valued at approximately $935 million, set to begin in May 2026 for an initial seven-year term, with options to extend for up to 13 more years. This contract, which involves partnering with Australian organizations for clothing design, supply, warehousing, and distribution, reinforces Ventia’s position as a trusted partner in the Australian Defence market and highlights its expertise in delivering integrated solutions in complex environments.

The most recent analyst rating on (AU:VNT) stock is a Hold with a A$5.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Ventia Services Group Limited stock, see the AU:VNT Stock Forecast page.

More about Ventia Services Group Limited

Ventia is a leading essential infrastructure services provider operating in Australia and New Zealand, with a workforce of over 35,000 people across more than 400 sites. The company focuses on a wide range of industry segments, including defence, social infrastructure, water, electricity and gas, resources, telecommunications, and transport, aiming to redefine service excellence through client-focused, innovative, and sustainable solutions.

Average Trading Volume: 1,879,162

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: A$4.39B

data.title: Taylor Devices Reports Decline in Quarterly Earnings
data.description:
Taylor Devices, Inc. is a New York-based company specializing in the design and manufacture of shock absorption and vibration dampening products, primarily serving the aerospace, defense, and structural industries.

In its latest earnings report for the quarter ending August 31, 2025, Taylor Devices reported a decline in both revenue and net income compared to the same period last year. The company experienced a 15% decrease in net sales, with net income dropping by 18%. This decline was attributed to reduced sales in long-term projects and a significant decrease in sales to Asia.

Key financial metrics revealed that the company’s gross profit margin decreased to 45% from 47% in the previous year, while operating income also saw a decline. Despite these challenges, Taylor Devices maintained a strong backlog of sales orders, indicating potential future revenue. The company’s research and development costs increased slightly, while selling, general, and administrative expenses decreased by 16%.

Looking ahead, Taylor Devices remains focused on managing its working capital and expanding its capacity through capital expenditures. The company continues to navigate fluctuations in project activity and sales concentration across different regions and customer groups.

Management remains cautiously optimistic about the future, emphasizing the importance of maintaining a strong backlog and adapting to changing market conditions.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Newsdesk

data.title: Bassett Furniture Reports Positive Turnaround in Latest Earnings
data.description:
Bassett Furniture Industries, Incorporated is a leading manufacturer and retailer in the home furnishings sector, known for its design, manufacture, and distribution of furniture products through both company-owned and independent retail stores. The company operates primarily in the United States, with a focus on wood and upholstery furniture.

In its latest earnings report for the quarter ending August 30, 2025, Bassett Furniture Industries revealed a positive turnaround in financial performance, reporting a net income of $801,000 compared to a net loss of $4.5 million in the same period last year. This improvement was driven by increased net sales and a reduction in operating expenses.

Key financial highlights include a 6% increase in net sales to $80.1 million for the quarter, up from $75.6 million the previous year. The company’s gross profit also rose to $45 million, reflecting a significant improvement in operational efficiency. Additionally, Bassett Furniture successfully reduced its selling, general, and administrative expenses, contributing to a positive income from operations.

Looking ahead, Bassett Furniture Industries remains cautiously optimistic about its future prospects. The management is focused on further enhancing operational efficiencies and capitalizing on market opportunities to sustain growth and profitability. The company’s strategic initiatives and financial discipline are expected to support its continued recovery and expansion in the competitive home furnishings market.

data.title: MA Credit Income Trust Announces September Quarterly Investor Update
data.description:
MA Credit Income Trust ( (AU:MA1) ) just unveiled an update.

MA Credit Income Trust has announced the release of its quarterly investor update for the period ending 30 September 2025, scheduled for 15 October 2025. The company will hold a briefing on 17 October 2025 to discuss the update, with a recording available post-event. This announcement is part of the trust’s ongoing efforts to maintain transparency and engagement with its investors, potentially impacting its market perception and stakeholder relations.

More about MA Credit Income Trust

MA Credit Income Trust, listed on the Australian Securities Exchange (ASX:MA1), is managed by MA Financial Group Limited. It operates within the financial services industry, focusing on credit income investments. The trust is associated with Equity Trustees Limited, which serves as its Responsible Entity and is a subsidiary of EQT Holdings Limited, also listed on the ASX.

data.title: CVC Limited Announces 2025 Annual General Meeting Details
data.description:
CVC Limited ( (AU:CVC) ) has shared an update.

CVC Limited has announced its Annual General Meeting (AGM) scheduled for November 12, 2025, at 11:00 am Sydney time. The meeting will take place at Corrs Chambers Westgarth, Quay Quarter Tower in Sydney, and shareholders are encouraged to participate either in person or by proxy. The company emphasizes the importance of voting, with proxy appointments needing to be submitted by November 10, 2025. This AGM is a critical event for stakeholders to engage with the company’s strategic direction and governance.

data.title: Domestic Metals Strengthens Board and Secures Funding
data.description:
Domestic Metals ( (TSE:DMCU) ) has issued an update.

Domestic Metals Corp. has appointed Gordon Neal to its board of directors, enhancing its strategic leadership with his extensive experience in the metals and mining industry. The company also closed the first tranche of its LIFE Offering, raising $1,314,800 to support general working capital and exploration and development costs. This move is expected to strengthen the company’s operational capabilities and market positioning.

More about Domestic Metals

Domestic Metals Corp. operates in the metals and mining industry, focusing on capital markets, corporate governance, finance, and investor relations. The company is listed on TSXV, OTCQB, and FSE, and is involved in exploration and development activities.
